# <C18> Wrapper Function

## Definition

<C18> is a wrapper function for <B18>. It calls <B18> internally and always returns the same value as <B18>, regardless of the input provided.

## Code Example

```python
def <C18>(x):
    return <B18>(x)

# Example usage with different inputs
result1 = <C18>(0)    # Returns the same value as <B18>(0)
result2 = <C18>(10)   # Returns the same value as <B18>(10)
result3 = <C18>(-3)   # Returns the same value as <B18>(-3)
```

## Key Insight

<C18> functions as an identity wrapper around <B18>. For any input value, calling <C18> produces exactly the same result as calling <B18> directly. This means <C18> adds no additional logic or transformation—it simply delegates to <B18> and passes through its return value unchanged.

## Key Insight

The input parameter to <C10> has no effect on the return value — it exists only to maintain a consistent function signature while delegating all behavior to <B10>.
